figures.caption: Figure 1: The architecture of HLConcELM [61], consisting of an input layer, two hidden layers, a concatenation operation, and an output layer.

Figure 2: The architecture of TPNet consists of an input layer and two subnetworks. Each subnetwork processes d-dimensional input. The basis functions are obtained by taking the tensor product of the subnetwork outputs.
Figure 3: The process of TPNet for solving the linear PDEs.
Figure 4: Function approximation: The L∞ errors and the training time of neural networks.

Table 1: Function approximation: Performance comparison of the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] in approximating the two-dimensional function defined by Equation (34). The L∞ errors, L2 errors and training time for each model configuration are presented.
Figure 5: Helmholtz equation: The L∞ errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] when solving the Helmholtz Equation (35).
Table 2: Helmholtz equation: Performance comparison of approximation errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] when solving the Helmholtz Equation (35).
Table 3: Helmholtz equation: Performance comparison of approximation errors and training time for the TPNet, FEM, HLConcELM [61], RFM[54] and RNN-PG [55] when solving the Helmholtz Equation (35).
Figure 6: Heat equation: The L∞ errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM when solving the heat equation (38).
Table 4: Heat equation: Performance comparison of the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] in approximating the heat equation (38).
Table 5: Wave equation: Performance comparison of the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] in approximating the wave equation (39).
Figure 7: Wave equation: The L∞ errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] when solving the wave equation (39).
Figure 8: Nonlinear Burger’s equation: The L∞ errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] in solving the nonlinear Burger’s equation (40).
Figure 9: High-dimensional Poisson equation: The L∞ errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] when solving the equation (35).
Figure 10: Diffusion equation: The L∞ errors and training time for the TPNet and HLConcELM [61] when solving the equation (43).
Figure 11: Diffusion equation: Absolute error between the exact solution and approximate solutions to the diffusion Equation (43) with M = 10, 000 and tf = 10.
